ddls:
CREATE TABLE "beliefs" (
  "belief_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each belief type */,
  "belief_description" TEXT /* Description of the belief type (e.g., 'Believe in God; absolutely certain') */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of belief (e.g., 'Theist', 'Atheist', 'Agnostic') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("belief_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the respondent */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the respondent */,
  "belief_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the belief type */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the respondent (e.g., 'High school', 'College', 'Graduate degree') */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the respondent (e.g., 'Low', 'Middle', 'High') */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_respondents_belief_id FOREIGN KEY ("belief_id") REFERENCES beliefs ("belief_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "surveys" (
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey */,
  "survey_name" TEXT /* Name of the survey */,
  "survey_description" TEXT /* Description of the survey */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the survey */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the survey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "survey_response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the survey */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the respondent */,
  "belief_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the belief type */,
  "harm_more" TEXT /* Percentage indicating belief does more harm than good */,
  "good_more" TEXT /* Percentage indicating belief does more good than harm */,
  "neither" TEXT /* Percentage indicating belief has no significant impact (neither) */,
  "unknown" TEXT /* Percentage of respondents that do not know */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("survey_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_belief_id FOREIGN KEY ("belief_id") REFERENCES beliefs ("belief_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survey_questions" (
  "question_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each question */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the survey */,
  "question_text" TEXT /* Text of the question */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("question_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_questions_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "answers" (
  "answer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each answer */,
  "survey_response_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the survey response */,
  "question_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the question */,
  "answer_text" TEXT /* Text of the answer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("answer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_answers_survey_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("survey_response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_answers_question_id FOREIGN KEY ("question_id") REFERENCES survey_questions ("question_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., 'Admin', 'Researcher', 'Analyst') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "user_access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user access entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the user */,
  "survey_id" INTEGER /* ID referencing the survey */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., 'Read', 'Write', 'Delete') */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_survey_id FOREIGN KEY ("survey_id") REFERENCES surveys ("survey_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each belief type
Description of the belief type (e.g., 'Believe in God; absolutely certain')
Education level of the respondent (e.g., 'High school', 'College', 'Graduate degree')
Unique identifier for each survey response
Percentage indicating belief does more harm than good
Percentage indicating belief does more good than harm
Percentage indicating belief has no significant impact (neither)
Percentage of respondents that do not know
Unique identifier for each user access entry
